title: Aura of uneasiness
content:
{{Top|fga|leg}} {{Update|[[Wretched Hives of Scum & Villainy]]}} {{ForcePower |image=[[File:OmminAndTheKetos-FNU.jpg]] |name=Aura of uneasiness |alternate=Shadow of uneasiness[[Dark Apprentice (novel)|''Dark Apprentice'']] |alignment=[[Dark side of the Force/Legends|Dark side of the Force]]''[[Tales of the Jedi Companion]]'' |area=[[Force power/Legends#Control|Control]] and [[Force power/Legends#Alter|Alter]] |purpose= |inventor= |affiliation=*[[Sith/Legends|Sith]] }} {{Quote|Aleema…I suddenly feel ''ill''.|Satal Keto suffering from King Ommin's aura of uneasiness|audio=SatalIll-TotJ.flac|[[Tales of the Jedi – The Freedon Nadd Uprising 2|''Tales of the Jedi – The Freedon Nadd Uprising'' 2]]}} An '''aura''' or '''shadow of uneasiness''' was an ancient [[Force power/Legends|technique]] conjured through [[Sith sorcery/Legends|Sith magic]] that allowed an individual to proceed unabated by projecting a field of vague unease around themselves. While [[Sentience/Legends|non-sentient]] creatures were primarily affected, cognizant beings also succumbed to its influence, experiencing feelings of confusion and discomfort when in close proximity to an individual emanating an aura of uneasiness. The technique was popular among [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]]–era [[Sith sorcerer|proponents of Sith magic]], and knowledge of the skill survived into the days of the [[New Republic/Legends|reinstated Galactic Republic]]. ==Description== {{Quote|…suddenly…everything's gone dark…I…can't think…''"
"''It's Sith magic, Wilum! Don't lose hope!|Cay Qel-Droma encourages Oss Wilum, as the latter suffers from Warb Null's aura of uneasiness|audio=AuraOfUneasiness-TotJ.flac|[[Tales of the Jedi (audio drama)|''Tales of the Jedi'' audio drama]]}} Using the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] to project an aura of uneasiness around oneself allowed a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient being]] to rid themselves of the nuisance of creatures that might otherwise cause them distress. When inside the aura's sphere of influence, animals and [[Insect/Legends|insects]] were compelled to avoid the path of the aura's projector, thus allowing the individual to proceed without delay. When performed in the presence of other sentient beings, the aura caused individuals to suffer from a sensation of intense malaise.[[Tales of the Jedi – The Freedon Nadd Uprising 2|''Tales of the Jedi – The Freedon Nadd Uprising'' 2]] [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitives]] experienced an expanding sense of darkness, as well as an inability to concentrate on whatever task they were attempting to perform at that moment.[[Tales of the Jedi – The Freedon Nadd Uprising 1|''Tales of the Jedi – The Freedon Nadd Uprising'' 1]] The ability was relatively simple to create and control, but its area of effect, although more potent when in close proximity to the point of origin, was restricted to the initiator's line of sight. ==Applications== [[File:NullvsJedi.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Jedi Knights are confounded by Warb Null's aura of uneasiness.]] Knowledge of projecting a shadow of uneasiness was widely dispersed among followers of ancient [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] philosophy. The skill was one of many among the repertoire of a seasoned [[Sith sorcerer]] and was an ability that they passed down to [[Sith apprentice/Legends|students]] of the dark side.In [[Dark Apprentice (novel)|''Dark Apprentice'']], Kyp Durron learned to create shadows of uneasiness from the spirit of the Dark Lord Exar Kun. The ancient [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Freedon Nadd]], during his reign as [[King/Legends|king]] of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Onderon/Legends|Onderon]], possessed the ability to create auras of unease.The ''[[Tales of the Jedi Companion]]'' states that Freedon Nadd knew every Force power included therein, among them being the aura of uneasiness. The talent survived him, remaining within the skill set of the sorcerous inheritors to Nadd's throne: King [[Ommin/Legends|Ommin]], and his [[Marriage/Legends|wife]] [[Amanoa]], [[Queen/Legends|Queen]] of Onderon. Ommin displayed the ability while meeting with [[Empress Teta system/Legends|Tetan]] cousins [[Aleema Keto|Aleema]] and [[Satal Keto/Legends|Satal Keto]], the latter of whom took suddenly ill when subjected to the power of the King's aura, while Aleema remained unaffected. The [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Warb Null]] also radiated an aura of uneasiness. [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Oss Wilum]] was especially affected during the [[Funeral of Amanoa|first battle]] of the [[Freedon Nadd Uprising|Naddist Uprising]], becoming disoriented and blinded by what he perceived as an all-consuming darkness. He was urged to resist the power of Null's aura by holding fast to the [[Light side of the Force/Legends|light side of the Force]]. ==Users== Auras of uneasiness were popular among the [[Naddist]] [[Sith cult/Legends|Sith cult]] that was active on Onderon during the planet's [[Beast Wars]]. Sith sorcerers such as King Ommin and Queen Amanoa, both Naddists and one-time members of the [[Iziz ruling family|Onderon royal family]],''[[The Dark Side Sourcebook]]'' displayed the ability to create them. The power was known and practiced by several Dark Lords of the Sith, such as Freedon Nadd and his successor, Exar Kun. Servants of the Sith, such as Freedon Nadd's guardian, Warb Null, emanated an aura of uneasiness while in the midst of combat. [[11 ABY/Legends|Many thousands of years]] after the Naddist Revolt, [[Padawan/Legends|Jedi trainee]]–turned–[[Sith/Legends|Sith]] [[Sith acolyte/Legends|acolyte]] [[Kyp Durron]] learned to create auras of uneasiness from the [[Force ghost/Legends|spirit]] of the [[Death/Legends|deceased]] Dark Lord [[Exar Kun/Legends|Exar Kun]], to whom Durron had recently apprenticed himself. He made a conscious effort to project a shadow of unease that kept the jungle fauna at bay as he moved through the forests of the [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin]] [[Moon/Legends|moon]]. The creatures sensed Durron's power and had no desire to move closer to him. ==Behind the scenes== The initial appearance of the shadow of uneasiness was in author [[Kevin J. Anderson]]'s [[July]] [[1994]] novel [[Dark Apprentice (novel)|''Dark Apprentice'']], second in [[Star Wars: The Jedi Academy Trilogy|''The Jedi Academy Trilogy'']]. It next appeared, albeit unnamed, the following month in the [[Tales of the Jedi – The Freedon Nadd Uprising 1|first issue]] of [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi (comic series)|''Tales of the Jedi''{{'s}}]] [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – The Freedon Nadd Uprising|''Freedon Nadd Uprising'']] story arc, and again in [[September]] of the same year with the release of the [[Tales of the Jedi – The Freedon Nadd Uprising 2|second installment]] of ''The Freedon Nadd Uprising''. [[1996|Two years later]], the Force power was detailed in [[West End Games]]' ''[[Tales of the Jedi Companion]]'', wherein it was referred to as the "aura of uneasiness." In terms of [[roleplaying game]] stats, the aura of uneasiness is an easily mastered and used Force power. Using the aura against another sentient player character automatically gives player characters a Dark Side point. ==Appearances== *[[Tales of the Jedi (audio drama)|''Tales of the Jedi'' audio drama]] *[[Tales of the Jedi – The Freedon Nadd Uprising 2|''Tales of the Jedi – The Freedon Nadd Uprising'' 2]] *''[[Darth Bane: Path of Destruction]]'' {{Ab|Darth Bane: Path of Destruction (audiobook)}} *[[Dark Apprentice (novel)|''Dark Apprentice'']] {{1st}} {{1stID|shadow of uneasiness}} *''[[The New Jedi Order: Traitor]]'' ==Sources== *''[[The Jedi Academy Sourcebook]]'' {{1stID|Aura of uneasiness}} *''[[Tales of the Jedi Companion]]'' *''[[Wretched Hives of Scum & Villainy]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} [[Category:Sith magic]]
